S.text: Glaucoma follow-up, on timolol BID OU.
Poor morning-dose adherence (~3-4 mornings/week); better with bedtime dose.
Denies respiratory symptoms/asthma with the beta-blocker.
New subjective left-sided peripheral vision loss (bumping into door frames on the left).

O.text: IOP 22 mmHg OD / 25 mmHg OS (GAT); up from 19/20 four months ago; baseline 28/30.
Optic nerves: C/D 0.7 OD stable; C/D 0.8 OS, worse than prior 0.75, with new inferior-rim disc hemorrhage OS.
HVF 24-2: deepening superior arcuate defect OS with worsened mean deviation vs 6 months ago; OD field stable.
OCT RNFL: new inferior RNFL thinning OS vs prior scan.

A.text: Primary open-angle glaucoma OU; OS progressing (structural and functional) with IOP above target; OD stable.

P.text: Continue timolol BID OU and add latanoprost one drop qHS OU as second agent.
Adherence intervention: phone alarms for the morning dose.
RTC 6 weeks for IOP recheck; repeat HVF in 3 months; consider laser trabeculoplasty if uncontrolled on two agents.
